DISCOVERY, 612, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER LIMITING PARTY DEPOSITIONS TO A SINGLE DAY OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, LIMITING THE SCOPE OF DEPOSITION QUESTIONING AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION AT DEPOSITION IS OFF CALENDAR. CALENDARING ERROR.

DISCOVERY 612, APPEARANCES: JIM WAGER-SMITH FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: JOSEPH GREEN JOSEPH L. SCHATZ FOR DEFENDANT AND OPPOSING PARTY: RALAN WONG HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER LIMITING PARTY DEPOSITIONS TO A SINGLE DAY, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, LIMITING THE SCOPE OF DEPOSITION QUESTIONING AND DOCUMENT PRODUCTION AT DEPOSITION IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER LIMITING THE DEPOSITION TO ONE DAY. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO OFFER ANY REASON FOR A DEPOSITION OF MORE THAN ONE DAY. WHILE THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED UPON A MOTION WHICH RAISED OBJECTIONS TO NUMEROUS DEMANDS MADE BY PLAINTIFF, THAT RULING DID NOT DETERMINE THAT THE SPECIFIC SUBJECT OF THIS MOTION WAS FOREVER REMOVED FROM CONSIDERATION. IT IS TELLING THAT NO ATTEMPT IS MADE TO DISCUSS THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE DEPOSITION WHICH MIGHT REQUIRE MORE THAN ONE DAY. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT
